Execution logs:
Here is the final answer from your managed agent 'search_agent':
### 1. Task outcome (short version):
The actor portraying Ray in the Polish-language version of "Everybody Loves Raymond" is Bartłomiej Kasprzykowski, who plays the character Roman.

### 2. Task outcome (extremely detailed version):
The Polish adaptation of "Everybody Loves Raymond" is titled "Wszyscy kochają Romana" (translating to "Everybody Loves Roman") and premiered on September 2, 2011. The show is a sitcom aired on the TVN network.

Bartłomiej Kasprzykowski stars as the lead character, Roman, who is a newspaper sportswriter, paralleling Ray Barone from the original American series. The cast also includes Aneta Todorczuk-Perchuć playing Dorota (the equivalent of Debra), Anna Seniuk as Maryla (Marie), Joachim Lamża as Zygmunt (Frank), and rapper Tede as Robert (Robert).

The show ran for only four episodes before being suspended due to low ratings, although there were plans to air additional episodes in a new time slot.

### 3. Additional context (if relevant):
The adaptation was part of the broader trend of creating localized versions of popular American sitcoms and aimed at capturing the same familial dynamics and humor that characterized the original series. However, like many adaptations, it faced challenges in resonating with the Polish audience as much as the original did with American viewers.

For more detail, find below a summary of this agent's work:
<summary_of_work>

Task outcome (short version):\nThe actor portraying Ray in the Polish-language version of "Everybody Loves Raymond" is Bartłomiej Kasprzykowski, who plays the character Roman.\n\n### 2. Task outcome (extremely detailed version):\nThe Polish adaptation of "Everybody Loves Raymond" is titled "Wszyscy kochają Romana" (translating to "Everybody Loves Roman") and premiered on September 2, 2011. The show is a sitcom aired on the TVN network.\n\nBartłomiej Kasprzykowski stars as the lead character, Roman, who is a newspaper sportswriter, paralleling Ray Barone from the original American series. The cast also includes Aneta Todorczuk-Perchuć playing Dorota (the equivalent of Debra), Anna Seniuk as Maryla (Marie), Joachim Lamża as Zygmunt (Frank), and rapper Tede as Robert (Robert).\n\nThe show ran for only four episodes before being suspended due to low ratings, although there were plans to air additional episodes in a new time slot.\n\n### 3. Additional context (if relevant):\nThe adaptation was part of the broader trend of creating localized versions of popular American sitcoms and aimed at capturing the same familial dynamics and humor that characterized the original series. However, like many adaptations, it faced challenges in resonating with the Polish audience as much as the original did with American viewers.'}]
